q: Species richness is defined as the number of species and abundance of each species that lives in a particular location. (2026)
Coral reefs have a very high biodiversity. (2025)
The diverse habitats are home for a wide range of genus. (2026)
The diverse habitats are home for a narrow range of species. (2024)
The diverse habitats are home for a wide range of species. (2024)
Rainfall strongly influences the distribution of different vegetation types. (2025)
Dry forest occurs where rainfall is usually more than 400 mm a year. (2025)
Forest ecosystems are a mineral sink and stabilize soils. (2025)
Geochelone platynota is the critically endangered species. (2025)
The common name of Linpai is Green Peafowl. (2025)
The destruction of the natural environment leads to habitat loss. (2025, 2024)
Habitat loss is the most significant cause of biodiversity loss globally. (2025)
Pollution is a growing threat on both land and in aquatic ecosystems. (2024)
Genetic pollution is desirable gene flow into wild populations. (2025)
Overfishing is the removal of a species of fish from a body of water at a rate greater than the species can replenish its population naturally. (2026)
Overhunting threatens one- third of endangered vertebrates. (2024)
Climate change is a growing threat to biodiversity. (2026, 2025)
Climate change is a growing threat to ecosystem. (2025)
Wildlife trade can involve the trade of living or dead individuals. (2024)
Wildlife smuggling directly affects the biodiversity of same ecosystems. (2024)
Conservation means keeping and protecting a living and changing environment. (2025)
Ex situ methods are the ways to conserve animals and plants outside their natural habitats. (2025)
Cryogenics technique used in captive breeding programmes. (2025)
The best way to conserve any species is to keep it in its natural habitat. (2025)
Ex situ, methods are the ways to conserve animals and plants inside their natural habitats. (2025)
A marine park is a permanent marine reservation for the conservation of species. (2025)
Artificial insemination technique used in captive breeding programmes. (2024)
